[must] Treat the design system as a Lighthouse-99 constraint with enforced governance: single bundled/purged/hashed CSS per surface, hard byte budgets in CI (public critical <=10KB inline; reject PRs that introduce arbitrary Tailwind values or push the bundle over budget), so 'comfortable UI' and 'Lighthouse 99 everywhere' provably coexist.
missedByOthers: No lens owns the metric-matched FONT FALLBACK contract (size-adjust/ascent-override/descent-override/line-gap-override). The perf lens demands CLS=0 and the spec (7.1) actually specifies font-display:swap — which is the direct CAUSE of font-swap reflow. Without a metric-matched fallback this is a guaranteed CLS hit on every text-heavy route. This must be a named, shared deliverable.

No one specified a 16px MINIMUM on form inputs. On iOS Safari and inside a Capacitor WKWebView, inputs under 16px trigger auto-zoom on focus — breaking the 'native feel' (packaging lens), the 'easy to use' mandate, and tap ergonomics. It's a one-line token rule with outsized UX impact.

env(safe-area-inset-*) handling is unaddressed. The packaging lens wants a native-feeling persistent shell in TWA/Capacitor but no one tokenized notch/home-indicator insets. Without inset padding tokens the bottom-tab-nav and top bar collide with the iOS home indicator and Android gesture bar — an immediate 'this is just a web wrapper' tell that also risks Apple's 4.2 review.

The security lens's strict CSP and my (and the perf lens's) inline-critical-CSS + Alpine-dynamic-styling needs were never reconciled at the STYLE-src level — everyone discussed script-src. The 'components toggle classes, never inline styles' rule is the missing bridge that lets style-src stay nonce-based; it has to be a design-system governance rule, not discovered late when CSP breaks the slot-grid.

Tabular numerals: nobody flagged that IDR prices, time slots, and the hold-countdown timer will visually jitter/misalign with proportional figures. A font-feature-settings 'tnum' token on all numeric contexts is required for both polish and CLS stability of the countdown.

Two-tier CSS delivery (inline route-critical slice + cached shared component sheet) is the concrete mechanism that resolves the perf lens's 'inline critical CSS per route' against my 'single hashed bundle for cache reuse' — neither lens stated how those coexist; without it you either bloat every page or lose first-paint speed.

resolvedTensions.conflict: PWA app-shell SPA vs SSR public surface (SEO/AI-crawler visibility)
resolvedTensions.decision: Hard-partition at the routing layer. Public routes (/, /{sport}/{city}, /venue/{slug}, /venue/{slug}/book) are independent SSR multi-page documents with ALL indexable facts (NAP, hours, price, AggregateRating, JSON-LD) in raw first-byte HTML — no JS/Livewire dependency, because AI crawlers (GPTBot/ClaudeBot/PerplexityBot) execute zero JS and Googlebot's two-wave render is unreliable. wire:navigate is allowed only as progressive-enhancement page transitions over already-complete documents. The persistent app-shell SPA (top bar, bottom tab nav, content region) lives ONLY behind /dashboard and /admin. Manifest start_url points at /dashboard (the installed-app surface is the operator tool), NOT the marketing homepage. Both surfaces share design tokens but are routed and packaged separately.

resolvedTensions.conflict: Service worker caching vs auth/tenant security vs SEO freshness vs frozen-wrapped-app
resolvedTensions.decision: Surface- and method-aware, self-healing SW. NEVER cache: /livewire/update, /dashboard/*, /admin/*, any non-GET, any response with Set-Cookie or Cache-Control:private, booking/availability JSON (network-only). Public SSR HTML = network-first short SWR passing through true HTTP status codes (200/301/404/410), never an app-shell fallback for public navigations. Content-hashed static assets (CSS/JS/font/AVIF) = cache-first immutable. App-shell precache = dashboard skeleton only, scoped so it never serves public routes. Cache named by build-id; activate deletes stale caches; skipWaiting+clients.claim gated behind in-app 'update available' prompt + kill-switch so a wrapped app never sticks on a stale shell.

resolvedTensions.conflict: Strict CSP (no unsafe-inline/unsafe-eval) vs inline critical CSS + Alpine/Livewire interactivity + full-page caching/TTFB
resolvedTensions.decision: Split by directive and surface. script-src stays strict everywhere: 'self' 'nonce-{n}' 'strict-dynamic', NO unsafe-inline, NO unsafe-eval — achieved via Livewire 3 CSP-safe build + @alpinejs/csp build (logic in Alpine.data(), never inline x-on expressions). Consolidate SW-register/map-facade/install-prompt into ONE hashed external JS (<5KB, deferred) so no inline script is needed. Public pages use a STATIC hash-based CSP (no per-request nonce) set at the edge/web-server so full-page HTTP caching and low TTFB are preserved; per-request nonces reserved for the already-dynamic dashboard. style-src 'self' 'nonce-{n}' with the inline critical-CSS block nonced; components toggle token utility CLASSES (:class), never inline style attributes (:style), and the hold-timer renders via a CSS custom property set through a class — so style-src needs no global 'unsafe-inline'. Lock sinks: connect-src 'self' https://*.xendit.co (+ capacitor://localhost and TWA host for the wrapper); img-src 'self' data:; frame-ancestors 'none'; base-uri/form-action 'self'; object-src 'none'; upgrade-insecure-requests. Verify JSON-LD ld+json survives the production CSP via Rich Results test in CI.

resolvedTensions.conflict: iOS thin web-wrapper (remote-origin WebView) vs Apple 4.2 + XSS blast radius
resolvedTensions.decision: iOS ships as a bundled Capacitor shell + versioned Sanctum (Bearer) JSON API, NOT Capacitor server.url pointing at the live origin (production-discouraged and the classic 4.2 rejection). Bundle the app-shell web build locally; use Capacitor OTA (Cap-go/CapacitorUpdater) for fast web-layer updates without re-review; add genuine native value (push, biometric unlock, native share, offline booking queue, secure storage) to clear minimum-functionality. Android TWA legitimately points at the same live origin (real Chrome, not a thin wrapper). One web codebase, packaged two ways: TWA same-origin (cookie+CSRF), iOS bundled+API (Bearer token).

resolvedTensions.conflict: Wrapper-safe Livewire auth vs hardened session cookies (SameSite)
resolvedTensions.decision: SameSite=Lax (NOT Strict) + Secure + HttpOnly with a fixed cookie domain (.venuepro.asia) so OTP/login redirects, Xendit return-URLs, and cold deep-link/notification opens through Chrome Custom Tabs and WKWebView keep the session. CSRF stays on all state-changing web/Livewire routes for the browser/TWA path; the iOS bundled app uses the Sanctum Bearer API for mutations so CSRF stays intact on web while the WebView carries a token. 'Auth round-trips successfully inside both a TWA Custom Tab and a Capacitor WKWebView' is an explicit acceptance gate, not a desktop-Chrome assumption.

resolvedTensions.conflict: Lighthouse 'seo' category vs real structured-data/NAP/canonical correctness
resolvedTensions.decision: Lighthouse seo>=0.99 is necessary but insufficient (it ignores JSON-LD correctness, canonical/hreflang, NAP). Add a SEPARATE per-route structured-data + meta validation CI gate alongside Lighthouse asserting: valid SportsActivityLocation (PostalAddress, geo, openingHoursSpecification, telephone, priceRange), AggregateOffer/Offer (IDR), AggregateRating from venue_reviews, BreadcrumbList, Organization+WebSite SearchAction on home; exactly one self-canonical; consistent og/twitter; <html lang="id">; plus a JS-disabled fetch asserting title/canonical/meta/single-h1/parseable JSON-LD present.

resolvedTensions.conflict: Interactive map (perf: off LCP/TBT path) vs map location crawlability (SEO)
resolvedTensions.decision:
Static AVIF map facade (click-to-load MapLibre via dynamic import) satisfies both because the indexable location signal is NOT the tiles — it is SSR text: full postal address in
+ PostalAddress/geo JSON-LD + a static map with descriptive alt. Crawlers read structured address; users click to load interactive tiles. Zero map JS on initial load (CI asserts this).

resolvedTensions.conflict: Plus Jakarta Sans brand font vs font-display:swap-induced CLS vs font byte budget
resolvedTensions.decision: Ship ONE variable woff2, Latin + Latin-Extended subset (covers all Bahasa Indonesia diacritics + tabular figures), weight axis 400-700, target <=40KB (design lens prefers <=20KB; cap at 40KB). Preload with crossorigin on every route. font-display:optional on the LCP heading (so a slow font never swaps mid-paint), swap elsewhere — both protected by a metric-matched @font-face fallback (size-adjust + ascent/descent/line-gap-override measured against Plus Jakarta Sans, local()-pinned to Roboto/SF) so swap contributes 0.000 to CLS. LCP element on public pages is the preloaded AVIF hero image, not text, keeping font swap off the LCP critical path. CI fails if >1 font file loads on a public route or subset exceeds 40KB.

resolvedTensions.conflict: Compact dashboard density (14px, tight padding) vs touch targets + accessibility
resolvedTensions.decision: Decouple VISUAL density from TOUCH density. 14px data text and tight visual padding allowed, but every interactive element keeps a >=44x44pt hit area via invisible hit-slop padding (slot grid renders small, taps big); 16px minimum on ALL form inputs (prevents iOS/WKWebView zoom-on-focus). focus-visible ring and the five booking-state tokens (hold/paid/confirmed/cancelled/no_show) are mandatory in both densities with verified AA contrast and each carries an icon/text label, never color alone.

resolvedTensions.conflict: Hard/unmissable refund disclosure vs comfortable UX + conversion + legal consent
resolvedTensions.decision: Progressive disclosure as a first-class tokenized component, not a fear-wall. Compact always-visible policy summary chip next to the price (calm caution status tokens at AA contrast, never alarm-red), tap-to-expand full policy, and a REQUIRED unchecked affirmative acknowledgement ('Saya mengerti') immediately above the pay button. Prominence comes from placement + required interaction, not visual aggression. The acknowledgement is SERVER-enforced (booking cannot transition to a payable charge without it) and audit-logged with policy version + timestamp + user — satisfying UU PDP lawful basis/non-repudiation AND conversion.

resolvedTensions.conflict: Public crawlable/fast discovery vs abuse/enumeration/scraping controls
resolvedTensions.decision: Apply controls asymmetrically by surface. Public SSR read routes: NO CAPTCHA, NO login wall, NO JS challenge on the LCP path — protect with HTTP/full-page caching, generous IP/ASN limits tuned above Googlebot+normal-user volume, and a reverse-DNS-verified crawler allowlist (Googlebot/Bingbot) + robots-governed AI crawlers (GPTBot/ClaudeBot/PerplexityBot) exempt from challenges. Concentrate strict controls (OTP composite rate-limits, hold/booking throttles, CAPTCHA-on-suspicion) ONLY on POST/auth/booking endpoints, which are noindex and off the LCP path. Same SSR HTML served to users and bots (no cloaking).

resolvedTensions.conflict: Persistent app-shell (native feel) vs main-thread/memory creep on low-end Android
resolvedTensions.decision: Bound the shell: @persist/wire:persist ONLY the static top bar and bottom-tab nav (no heavy state); wire:navigate fully tears down and rebuilds the content-region component each navigation so Alpine instances and event listeners are GC'd. Never @persist content. Dashboard JS frozen at 80KB core; no per-route JS added inside the shell. CI audits a dashboard route in TWO states — cold load AND after >=5 scripted wire:navigate hops (steady-state) — plus a SW-warmed state, so TBT/INP/memory degradation is caught.

resolvedTensions.conflict: Lens-mandated booking affordances (timer, optimistic feedback) vs perf budget (TBT/CLS)
resolvedTensions.decision: Classify every affordance as client-instant or server-authoritative. Press feedback, selected/disabled state, and the hold countdown timer are pure Alpine (CSP build) + CSS transform/opacity — already in the budgeted 80KB core, zero network, ~0ms TBT. Only authoritative availability + the hold is a debounced Livewire round-trip, optimistically reflected first. CLS=0 preserved by SSR-reserving slot-grid and timer boxes with fixed dims and tabular-nums. Rule: user feedback is always client-instant, truth is always server-authoritative.

resolvedTensions.conflict: Per-route inline critical CSS (perf) vs single hashed bundle for cache reuse (design system)
resolvedTensions.decision: Two-tier delivery from one @theme token source. Tokens defined once; Tailwind v4 only emits a variable when referenced, so two physically separate purged bundles are built — a public bundle (purged against Blade/SSR, dark-mode + dashboard utilities absent) and a dashboard bundle (loaded only behind auth, never on a public Lighthouse run). Per route: inline a tiny route-critical slice (tokens + above-fold layout, <=10KB) AND <link> the shared hashed component sheet (free on navigation 2+). Public critical CSS budget <=10KB inline; CI rejects arbitrary Tailwind values and bundle-over-budget PRs.

sections.seo:
SEO & ANSWER-ENGINE OPTIMIZATION (spec addition)

SSR-first, JS-independent public surface [must]. Every public route — /, /{sport}/{city} (e.g. /padel/jakarta-selatan), /{sport}/{city}/{venue-slug}, /venue/{slug}, /venue/{slug}/book — must serve ALL indexable content in raw server-rendered HTML on first byte: venue name, NAP/postal address, geo, operating hours, price/AggregateOffer, AggregateRating, breadcrumbs, and all JSON-LD. Zero dependency on JavaScript/Livewire/wire:navigate to populate content. Rationale: AI/answer crawlers (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot) execute NO JS in 2026 and Googlebot's two-wave render is unreliable. wire:navigate allowed only as a transition over independently-complete SSR documents.

Structured data (server-rendered JSON-LD, validated in CI) [must]: SportsActivityLocation { name, PostalAddress, geo {lat,lng}, openingHoursSpecification, telephone, priceRange, image }, Offer/AggregateOffer in IDR, AggregateRating + Review from venue_reviews, BreadcrumbList, FAQPage on venue pages (court types, pricing bands, cancellation, amenities — AEO extraction surface), Organization + WebSite with SearchAction on home. Exactly one self-referential <link rel=canonical>; consistent og:/twitter:; <html lang="id">.

SEO validation as a CI gate SEPARATE from Lighthouse [must]: Lighthouse seo>=0.99 is necessary but insufficient (ignores JSON-LD correctness, canonical/hreflang, NAP). Run a structured-data validator (Rich Results rules) + meta/canonical linter per route, plus a JS-disabled fetch asserting <title>, single self-canonical, meta description, exactly one <h1>, and parseable JSON-LD on every public route.

URL/crawl architecture [must]: /{sport}/{city} district pages are primary local-landing pages (unique localized Bahasa copy, gated by the liquidity threshold so no thin pages publish). Chunked lastmod-accurate sitemap index (venues, district, sport) regenerated on venue create/update. robots.txt: allow verified search + AI crawlers on public GET; disallow /dashboard, /admin, booking-confirmation, and all faceted filter-permutation query params (crawl traps); canonical-to-base for non-indexable facets; indexable facets use crawlable <a href> links, never JS-only.

Local SEO is two-sided [must]: GBP (~32%) + proximity (~55%) + reviews (~16-20%) dominate the local pack. Venue onboarding + admin captures/links each venue's Google Business Profile and enforces NAP/hours/category consistency across site+GBP+IG/WA (VenuePro acts as a NAP-consistency engine); admin surfaces a 'claim/verify GBP' checklist. Consistent NAP rendered as SSR <address> + PostalAddress JSON-LD matching GBP exactly.

Reviews as a ranking asset [must]: render review count/score as crawlable SSR text + AggregateRating schema; post-play WA/email prompt (reuses the notification pipeline) grows review velocity. Render a short crawlable cancellation-policy summary on the public venue page (SSR static text, zero JS/perf cost) to pre-set expectations and protect rating quality.

i18n future-proofing [should]: ship <html lang="id"> in v1; design URL/template layer for EN-later via /en/ path-prefix with reciprocal hreflang (id<->en) + x-default + per-locale self-canonical. Never geo-redirect or auto-translate. IDR/WIB in schema and visible text.

No cloaking; crawler-aware security (co-owned with Security) [should]: same SSR HTML to users and bots; reverse-DNS-verified crawler allowlist exempt from JS-challenge/rate-limits on public GET only; challenges apply solely to POST/auth/OTP. Verify production CSP does not strip <script type="application/ld+json"> (Rich Results test in CI).

CWV are a ranking signal [should]: the per-route Lighthouse>=0.99 matrix IS an SEO requirement — CLS=0, LCP<2.5s, font subset by actual Bahasa/IDR character set (no tofu), >=16px body on public 'airy' density (14px confined to noindex dashboard).
sections.pwa_app:
PWA & MOBILE-APP READINESS (spec addition)

Two separate routing surfaces, one codebase [must]: public discovery/venue/booking = independent SSR multi-page documents (deep-linked INTO, ranked, never the installed-app home). Authenticated Livewire dashboard+admin behind /dashboard and /admin = persistent app-shell SPA (static top bar, bottom tab nav, content region, wire:navigate, zero full-page reloads inside the shell). Shared design tokens, separately routed and packaged. Web PWA is the product; native shells add only deltas behind capability detection (window.Capacitor?.isNativePlatform(), display-mode media query, android-app:// referrer) — never a forked UI.

Complete packaging-ready Web App Manifest in v1 [must]: serve /manifest.webmanifest (correct MIME, same origin) with name, short_name, id, start_url '/dashboard?source=pwa' (installed app opens to the operator tool, NOT marketing home), scope '/', display standalone, display_override [standalone,minimal-ui], orientation portrait, theme_color + background_color (brand tokens), lang id, dir ltr, categories [sports,business], self-hosted icons 192/512 + dedicated 512 maskable (safe-zone padded) + monochrome notification badge, shortcuts (Today's bookings, New walk-in, Credit top-up), narrow+wide screenshots. These sit off every route's critical path (tracked in a separate 'install assets' budget bucket) — ~0 Lighthouse perf cost. Validate manifest completeness/installability in CI (Bubblewrap/PWABuilder + Lighthouse PWA checks).

Android TWA readiness [must]: serve /.well-known/assetlinks.json from a Laravel ROUTE (never SW-cached) with relation delegate_permission/common.handle_all_urls, package asia.venuepro.app, and an ARRAY of SHA-256 fingerprints (upload key + Play App Signing from the Play Console). Keep a checked-in twa-manifest.json/Bubblewrap config (host, name, theme/background color, start_url /dashboard?source=twa, iconUrl=512 maskable). TWA points at the same live origin scoring Lighthouse 99 (real Chrome, not a thin wrapper). Plan for the May-2026 Play closed-testing rule (12 testers / 14 days) in the launch timeline.

iOS = bundled Capacitor shell + versioned API, NOT remote-origin WebView [must]: do NOT use Capacitor server.url against the live site (production-discouraged, the classic 4.2 thin-wrapper rejection). Bundle the app-shell web build locally; talk to Laravel via a versioned Sanctum (Bearer) JSON API for data/mutations; use Capacitor OTA (Cap-go/CapacitorUpdater) for fast web-layer updates without re-review; set limitsNavigationsToAppBoundDomains. Add genuine native value to clear Apple 4.2: APNs push, biometric unlock, native share/add-to-calendar, haptics, offline booking queue, secure storage. Same Livewire/Blade app-shell — one codebase, packaged two ways.

Wrapper-safe auth & session [must]: session/auth cookies Secure; HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax (NOT Strict) with fixed domain .venuepro.asia so OTP/redirect/Xendit-return/deep-link handoffs through Custom Tabs and WKWebView keep the session. CSRF stays on all web/Livewire state-changing routes for browser/TWA; iOS bundled app uses the Bearer API for mutations. Extend CSP connect-src/form-action to include capacitor://localhost and the TWA host without loosening script-src. ACCEPTANCE GATE: auth round-trips successfully inside both a TWA Custom Tab and a Capacitor WKWebView (not a desktop-Chrome assumption).

Surface/method-aware self-healing service worker [must]: NEVER cache /livewire/update, /dashboard/*, /admin/*, any non-GET, any Set-Cookie/Cache-Control:private response, or booking/availability JSON (network-only). Precache + SWR ONLY public SSR routes, hashed Vite assets, self-hosted fonts, static map facade. Public HTML = network-first passing through true 200/301/404/410 (never an app-shell fallback for public navs). Hashed static = cache-first immutable. App-shell precache = dashboard skeleton only, scoped off public routes. Cache named by build-id; activate evicts stale; skipWaiting+clients.claim behind an in-app 'update available' toast + kill-switch + version header so a wrapped app never sticks on a stale shell. Livewire owns intra-shell navigation; SW owns only the offline fallback + static shell.

Deep/universal links defined now [must]: canonical https patterns (/{sport}/{city}/{venue-slug}, /venue/{slug}, /venue/{slug}/book, /dashboard/bookings/{id}, /pay/{ref}) round-trip identically in browser, TWA (handle_all_urls + autoVerify intent-filter), and iOS (Universal Links). Reserve /.well-known/apple-app-site-association (application/json, no extension, same origin, Team ID + bundle ID appLinks paths) now. Every server route renders a valid standalone page for every deep-linkable URL (cold deep-link never 404s); push payloads carry a deep_link.

Push as a swappable token source [should]: push_subscriptions (user_id, platform [webpush|fcm|apns], endpoint/token, keys, ua, last_seen) + provider-agnostic Notification channel so booking-confirmation/reminder/low-credit events fan out to push exactly as to WA/email. Web push now: VAPID, SW push+notificationclick (clients.openWindow to deep_link), permission prompt fired only from a user gesture and primed after the first successful booking. Native later: Capacitor delivers FCM/APNs tokens into the same table — no business-logic change. Server keys vaulted.

App-readiness baked into the design system [should]: env(safe-area-inset-*) padding tokens on top bar + bottom nav (inert on web, load-bearing in the wrapper); dashboard honors prefers-color-scheme and drives theme-color/status-bar; hard 44x44pt hit area on slot-grid/nav controls even in compact density; install/handoff UX with source attribution (web vs TWA vs iOS). Offline write-intent: an offline QUEUE for walk-in entry / status changes that re-validates server-side on reconnect (the DB EXCLUDE constraint is the integrity backstop).
sections.lighthouse99: LIGHTHOUSE >=99 PER-ROUTE PLAN (spec addition) Per-route CI gate at 0.99 (replaces spec's '>=98 on key pages') [must]: @lhci/cli (0.15.x) autorun, mobile Moto-G-class preset (CPU 4x throttle, 1.6Mbps, 150ms RTT), numberOfRuns:3 median, GitHub Actions matrix (one URL per leg) against a seeded staging build (3-8 anchor venues so discovery/map render real volume). Audit EVERY route class — /, /{sport}/{city}, /venue/{slug}, /venue/{slug}/book, plus a representative authed /dashboard route (seeded session cookie) — asserting performance, accessibility, best-practices, AND seo each >=0.99 via assertMatrix. Audit the dashboard route in THREE states: cold load, after >=5 scripted wire:navigate hops (steady-state SPA), and SW-warmed (returning visitor). Any route/state <0.99 fails the pipeline. Per-template asset budgets, CI-enforced (budgets.json + size-limit) [must]: public SSR routes <=30KB JS (br), <=14KB critical CSS inline (design-system targets <=10KB) + deferred remainder, LCP image <=90KB AVIF, ONE font file <=40KB. Dashboard <=80KB JS (Livewire+Alpine core) + per-Livewire-component HTML payload <=40KB. Every route: TBT<150ms, CLS=0, LCP<2.5s throttled. resourceCounts cap third-party=0. Vite manualChunks split so Alpine/Livewire never load on public SSR routes — CI fails if a public entrypoint imports the Livewire runtime (size-limit per entrypoint catches stray imports before Lighthouse runs). LCP discipline [must]: all venue/court imagery as responsive AVIF (WebP fallback) at 320/640/960/1280 via /srcset/sizes, every with explicit width/height (stored on venue_images: width,height columns — required so SSR emits dimensions and CLS=0), self-hosted, no CDN. LCP element per public route is a preloaded image (fetchpriority=high, ), not a text headline, so font swap is off the LCP path. Below-fold lazy + decoding=async + fetchpriority=low. Server-side image pipeline (Intervention/Spatie Media Library) transcodes on upload, strips EXIF, AVIF q~50-60 (hero<=90KB, card<=30KB). Map = static AVIF facade [must]: visible element is a self-hosted static map image with play-button overlay; MapLibre GL + self-hosted Jakarta PMTiles dynamically imported only on user click. CI asserts ZERO map JS on initial load of discovery and venue pages. Zero render-blocking + font-swap CLS=0 [must]: inline per-template critical CSS, defer the rest; ONE Plus Jakarta Sans variable woff2 (Latin+Latin-Ext, axis 400-700, <=40KB), preloaded crossorigin; font-display:optional on the LCP heading, swap elsewhere; metric-matched @font-face fallback (measured size-adjust/ascent-override/descent-override/line-gap-override against local() Roboto/SF) so swap contributes 0.000 to CLS. CI fails if >1 font file loads on a public route or subset exceeds 40KB. CSP at zero perf cost [must]: NO inline scripts — consolidate SW-register/map-facade/install-prompt into one hashed deferred external JS (<5KB) so script-src needs neither unsafe-inline nor a per-request nonce on public pages; @alpinejs/csp build so no unsafe-eval. Public CSP is hash-based, STATIC, set at the edge with HSTS/Referrer-Policy/Permissions-Policy(geolocation=(self)) so it never blocks full-page caching or raises TTFB; per-request nonces only on the dynamic dashboard. No COEP/COOP (self-hosted; would risk the map facade/wrapper). App-shell without main-thread creep [must]: @persist/wire:persist ONLY top bar + bottom nav; wire:navigate fully tears down/rebuilds the content component each nav (never @persist content) so Alpine/listeners GC and TBT/INP/memory stay flat across the session. No per-route JS inside the shell. The steady-state and SW-warmed audits above prove the wrapped app stays at 99. Livewire partial-render CLS [must]: booking calendar reserves fixed box dimensions for slot grid, price, and hold-timer region (tabular-nums) so a Livewire morph/round-trip never shifts layout. SSR the initial day's slot grid (LCP/FCP don't wait on a round-trip); scope the Livewire component to render only the slot-grid partial (<40KB); wire:navigate.hover prefetch on venue->book. Cheap-paint visuals on hot surfaces [should]: 2-step single-layer shadow scale, blur<=8px, NO box-shadow on frequently re-rendered elements (slot cells, Livewire-refreshed rows — use 1px border + bg tint). Dark-mode + dashboard-density utilities absent from public critical CSS (gated behind classes never present in the public DOM). TTFB/LCP floor [should]: Laravel Octane + FrankenPHP worker mode (audit singletons for cross-request state leaks, --max-requests to bound memory, load-test before relying on it); Redis hot-availability cache; short-TTL full-response/fragment cache for SSR discovery/venue keyed by sport+city+filters; brotli + immutable long-cache on hashed assets; HTTP/2; same-origin preconnect only (zero external). Policy locks: no third-party JS on public SSR routes ever (analytics = first-party hashed deferred beacon); add field RUM/CrUX monitoring post-launch (lab !== field on low-end Jakarta 4G).
sections.security:
SECURITY & OWASP CONTROLS (spec addition — Section 7.4)

Webhook is the only money mutator — unforgeable, idempotent, replay-proof, tenant-bound [must]: verify Xendit x-callback-token via hash_equals (constant-time). CRITICAL: x-callback-token is a STATIC shared token, not a per-message HMAC — token alone is insufficient, so additionally re-fetch/assert the payment by provider_ref AND match the event's sub-account/business_id to the booking's venue_id (reject mismatch). INSERT event_id into webhook_events (UNIQUE) BEFORE side-effects (idempotency). Enforce an allowed-transition matrix via conditional UPDATE ... WHERE status='hold' AND hold_expires_at>now() (0 rows = late/duplicate). Re-validate amount_idr + currency against the stored booking total (blocks amount-tampering). Route is CSRF-exempt but IP-allowlisted to Xendit ranges, no auth-session, fast 200 + queued side-effects. Credit deduction and any venue refund happen ONLY inside this verified idempotent path.

xenPlatform for-user-id confused-deputy control (#1 unnamed money-IDOR) [must]: the platform master key can act on ANY sub-account via for-user-id. Centralize ALL Xendit calls in one gateway service that derives for-user-id strictly from the route-bound authorized venue_id (never request input), asserting the target sub-account belongs to the acting tenant before any charge/refund. Refunds are policy-authorized, OTP/re-auth-gated, idempotent (keyed by booking), server-compute the refund % from the CancellationPolicy (player cannot influence), with their own audit row; alert on abnormal refund volume per venue. A late/0-row 'paid' event must NOT auto-trigger a refund unless the payment actually settled to that sub-account, exactly once.

Strict split nonce/hash CSP via shared middleware [must]: script-src 'self' 'nonce-{n}' 'strict-dynamic' — NO unsafe-inline, NO unsafe-eval (Livewire 3 CSP-safe build + @alpinejs/csp). Public pages use STATIC hash-based script-src (cacheable, no per-request nonce). style-src 'self' 'nonce-{n}' on the inline critical-CSS block; components toggle token CLASSES, never inline :style. connect-src 'self' https://*.xendit.co (+ capacitor://localhost, TWA host); img-src 'self' data:; default-src 'self'; base-uri/form-action 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none'; object-src 'none'; upgrade-insecure-requests; Trusted Types where supported. Verify ld+json survives CSP via Rich Results in CI. Same baseline header pipeline for SSR + dashboard.

OTP/WhatsApp abuse — SMS-pumping, brute-force, enumeration [must]: composite-key limiters — 'otp-send' Limit::perMinute(1)->by(phone) AND daily cap (~5/day/phone) AND per-IP (~10/hr) AND per-subnet/device — with exponential cooldowns; a channel-cost circuit breaker (hard daily WA send budget + spend alerts, auto-throttle on volume spikes). OTPs 6-digit, hashed (HMAC), single-use, 5-min TTL, max 5 verify attempts then invalidate+force resend (Redis keyed by E.164-normalized phone so 08.../+62... can't bypass). Constant-time identical responses for existing/non-existing accounts (no enumeration). Throttle UI = SSR'd disabled button + server-rendered countdown placeholder (CLS=0, no extra JS). CAPTCHA/Turnstile after threshold, on the POST endpoint only — never on the SSR/LCP path. Re-issue session ID on login; OTP re-challenge for refund execution, credit top-up, phone change.

Multi-tenancy / IDOR [must]: bind active venue from the route (not a global scope, never venue_id from request body); Policies on bookings/payments/credits/refunds/reviews/settings checking $user->venue_id===$model->venue_id; Gate::before grants platform_admin ONLY and must not leak into tenant read models (public discovery deliberately un-scoped, dashboard strictly scoped); resolve children via parent ($venue->bookings()->findOrFail($id)). In Livewire mark ownership props #[Locked] and re-authorize inside every action (public properties are untrusted). Blocking Pest isolation suite: staff of Venue A get 403/404 on every Venue B resource and money action.

Mass-assignment, validation, raw-PostGIS SQLi [must]: explicit FormRequests/DTOs, never request()->all(); guard role, venue_id, status, balance, xendit_subaccount_id, price_idr, service_fee_idr, rating_avg (set server-side from auth context). PostGIS ST_DWithin/geography + full-text always use bound parameters (never interpolate lat/lng/radius/search); cast numerics, clamp radius max, allowlist sport/city/slug. Server recomputes price + service fee + credit cost authoritatively at hold/confirm (no client-trusted amounts).

File upload, SSRF, headers [must]: venue images — finfo magic-byte + extension allowlist (jpg/png/webp), size cap, re-encode/strip EXIF (GPS/PII), store off web-root with random names, serve via signed/temporary URLs, no SVG unless sanitized. SSRF on any server-side fetch / venue-supplied URL (website/IG/WA/image): https-only, allowlist hosts, block RFC1918/link-local/169.254.169.254, no redirects to internal. Xendit return URLs server-constructed from booking id (never reflected from query param); deep links map a fixed scheme to internal routes (a deep link is a URL, re-authorized server-side, not an authz bypass); external links rel=noopener noreferrer. CORS first-party only. Headers: HSTS preload, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin, minimal Permissions-Policy, force HTTPS.

Wrapper-safe session/SW (cross-cuts PWA) [must]: cookies HttpOnly+Secure+SameSite=Lax (.venuepro.asia); CSRF on all web/Livewire state-changing routes; SW caches ONLY versioned public assets + public shell — never /livewire/update, never auth-middleware routes, never any response carrying the session cookie/Set-Cookie (network-only for authenticated + all POST). Capacitor uses the Sanctum Bearer API for mutations. assetlinks.json + AASA pinned to exact signing-cert SHA-256 + app-id.

PII / UU PDP (Law 27/2022, fines up to 2% revenue) [must]: documented lawful basis per purpose (contract for booking; separate granular withdrawable consent for WA marketing/push, logged with timestamp). Encrypt phone numbers + booking history at rest (encrypted casts + blind-index/HMAC for lookup); mask phone in UI/logs (last 3-4 digits). Data-subject rights flows (access/export, rectification, erasure, consent withdrawal) with SLAs, retaining minimal financial/audit records. 72-hour breach-notification runbook (DPA + subjects). Auto-purge stale OTPs, raw webhook payloads, abandoned holds. Treat Xendit + WA BSP as processors (DPAs). Scrub PII from error tracking/log aggregation. Device push tokens are personal data — in retention/export/erasure scope; auth+tenant-bound, minimal payload, keys vaulted.

Consent/disclosure server-enforced [must]: the refund/fee acknowledgement is an affirmative, unchecked-by-default, server-gated, versioned, audit-logged consent event (policy version + timestamp + user) — booking cannot transition to a payable charge without it.

Booking-flow business-logic abuse [should]: rate-limit hold creation per user/IP (anti hold-spam/inventory-griefing), cap concurrent active holds, require a verified (OTP) session before a paid hold, robust expired-hold release job (queue+retry, cancels the pending Xendit charge), credit-deduct + refund-reversal atomic and idempotent (exactly once, never negative/double-spent), walk-in path through the same EXCLUDE + tenant policy, 'insufficient credits -> bookings paused' fails safe.

Audit logging + secrets/supply-chain [should]: append-only, externally-shipped, tamper-evident trail for money + security events (OTP send/verify, login/failure, role/permission change, settings/policy edits, credit topup/deduct/reversal, refund executed, consent given, webhook receipt+decision with event_id, admin elevation) — actor/tenant/IP/UA/timestamp + before/after, PII masked. Secrets (Xendit master + sub keys, x-callback-token, WA BSP, DB/Redis, APP_KEY, VAPID) vaulted + rotated, per-environment, never in repo/manifest/client; Redis password-protected, not network-exposed. composer audit + npm audit/Dependabot + Enlightn in CI; pin Xendit SDK + spatie/laravel-permission; APP_DEBUG=false; Telescope/Horizon behind auth+IP allowlist.
sections.ux:
UX & USABILITY PRINCIPLES (spec addition)

Booking-flow ergonomics + instant feedback [must]: discover->slot->pay completes in minimal taps with feedback within 100ms of every tap. Classify every affordance — slot press, selected/disabled state, hold countdown are client-instant (Alpine CSP build + CSS transform/opacity, zero network, ~0ms TBT); only availability truth and the hold are server-authoritative (debounced Livewire, optimistic UI first). Reserve slot-grid and timer boxes at SSR (fixed dims, tabular-nums) so CLS=0. On a 23P01 'slot just taken' conflict, show a calm inline message + auto-refresh the grid — never a raw error or dead end. Rule: user feedback is always client-instant, truth is always server-authoritative.

Touch, tap-target & safe-area [must]: every interactive element >=44x44pt hit area (invisible hit-slop where the visible chip is compact, e.g. dashboard slot grid) with >=8px spacing; touch-action:manipulation. All fixed chrome (public sticky CTA, dashboard bottom tab nav) padded with env(safe-area-inset-*) to clear notch/Dynamic Island/gesture bar in browser, TWA, Capacitor. Bottom nav capped at 5 top-level items (Calendar, Bookings, Credits, Reviews, More), icon+label, active highlighted.

Accessibility WCAG 2.2 AA as a Lighthouse-99 co-requirement [must]: the 0.99 gate is ALL FOUR categories. focus-visible ring on every interactive element (tokenized, AA in both densities); semantic landmarks + sequential headings; aria-label on icon-only buttons; booking-state and all functional color carry an icon/text label (never color alone); form errors via role=alert/aria-live; prefers-reduced-motion respected; Dynamic Type / text-scaling without truncation.

Form & input UX (the OTP/phone + payment choke point) [must]: visible labels (not placeholder-only); inputmode/type=tel for phone, numeric + autocomplete=one-time-code for OTP (autofills from SMS/WA); >=16px font on all inputs (no iOS/WKWebView zoom); inline validation on blur not keystroke; errors below the field with cause+fix; auto-focus first invalid field; loading+disabled on every async submit. OTP error copy is single, friendly, non-revealing AND actionable with a visible resend cooldown ('Jika nomor terdaftar, kode telah dikirim · Kirim ulang 0:30') — satisfies anti-enumeration without stranding users; lockout shows a calm 'coba lagi dalam X menit' + WhatsApp-support fallback, never a dead end.

Refund/cancellation disclosure as progressive disclosure [must]: compact always-visible policy summary chip next to the price (calm caution status tokens, not alarm-red), tap-to-expand full venue policy, and a REQUIRED unchecked acknowledgement ('Saya mengerti kebijakan venue') immediately above the pay button — server-enforced + audit-logged. Prominence via placement + required interaction, not visual aggression. Public venue page shows a short crawlable policy summary too.

State preservation, deep-linking & back integrity [must]: filter/sort/date/tab state lives in the URL query string on public discovery AND the dashboard, so wire:navigate back, refresh-inside-wrapper, share, and deep links restore scroll+state losslessly (co-serves PWA deep-link + SEO shareable-URL). Configure wire:navigate to preserve scroll. Multi-field dashboard forms auto-save drafts so accidental wrapper dismissal never loses work.

Trust, loading, empty & offline states [must]: skeleton/shimmer (not blank spinners) for any wait >300ms; clear booking-confirmation success state with what-happens-next (WA confirmation incoming); helpful empty states with a next action; visible 'tersedia per HH:MM' timestamp on SWR availability so users never act on stale data; calm offline fallback; confirmation dialog before destructive actions (cancel booking, issue refund) with undo where feasible.

Two densities, one token contract [must]: airy public for conversion/trust; compact dashboard for operator throughput — density is VISUAL only; touch density (>=44pt), AA contrast, focus rings, and the five booking-state tokens (each icon+label) are identical across both. Bahasa Indonesia copy, IDR tabular figures, WIB times, locale-aware formatting. Plain-language microcopy, no jargon.

Launch-blocking UX gaps to own: (1) cold-start/empty-inventory — when radius search returns 0-2 venues show 'nearest cluster + WhatsApp the venue', never a blank/broken page. (2) Walk-in/staff entry — a one-screen, few-tap walk-in booking through the same EXCLUDE constraint with instant conflict feedback (must beat pen-and-paper or adoption fails). (3) Notification UX — an in-app notifications inbox (new booking, low credit, refund request) + permission-priming (never fire the native push prompt cold; prime after the first successful booking — this also clears Apple 4.2). (4) Hold-timer expiry — visible calm countdown, T-2min nudge, graceful 'hold expired, slot offered again if still free' recovery. (5) i18n copy as a reviewed string catalogue (Bahasa primary), non-revealing where security requires — never improvised in templates.
sections.ui_design_system:
UI DESIGN-SYSTEM TOKENS (spec addition — single Tailwind v4 @theme source)

GOVERNANCE [must]: define ALL tokens once in an @theme layer; Tailwind v4 emits a CSS variable only when referenced. Build TWO purged/hashed bundles — public (purged against Blade/SSR; dark-mode + dashboard-density utilities absent) and dashboard (loaded only behind auth, never on a public Lighthouse run). Every visual value comes from a token; NO arbitrary bracket values (CI lint rejects them). Two-tier delivery: inline a route-critical slice (<=10KB) + <link> the shared hashed component sheet (free on nav 2+). CI: public critical CSS <=10KB inline, reject over-budget PRs.

TYPEFACE [must]: Plus Jakarta Sans (Indonesian-designed, full Bahasa Latin diacritics, tabular figures), self-hosted via @fontsource — NOT Google Fonts/CDN. ONE variable woff2, Latin+Latin-Ext subset, weight axis 400-700, <=40KB (prefer <=20KB after subsetting via glyphhanger/pyftsubset; keep tabular-figure glyphs). @font-face font-display:optional on the LCP heading, swap elsewhere, with unicode-range; preload ONLY the critical weight (crossorigin). Mandatory metric-matched fallback: size-adjust + ascent-override + descent-override + line-gap-override (Fontaine/capsize-computed) against local() Roboto (Android/TWA) / SF (iOS) so swap CLS = 0.000. Token --font-sans: "Plus Jakarta Sans", var(--font-fallback).

TYPE SCALE [must] (@theme --text-* with baked line-height/letter-spacing/weight): --text-xs 12/16 (labels, meta); --text-sm 14/20 (dashboard body + table cells — dashboard default); --text-base 16/24 (public body + ALL inputs, 16px min prevents iOS/WKWebView zoom); --text-lg 18/26; --text-xl 20/28; --text-2xl 24/32; --text-3xl 30/36; --text-4xl 36/40; --text-5xl 48/1.05 (public hero). letter-spacing -0.01em on >=2xl, 0 on body. Weights: normal 400, medium 500 (UI/buttons), semibold 600 (headings/prices/court names), bold 700 (hero). font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums on all price/time/IDR/countdown contexts (alignment + countdown CLS stability). IDR via a @rupiah Blade helper / Number::currency as 'Rp 250.000' (id-ID dot grouping).

SPACING [must]: 4px base / 8px rhythm (Tailwind --spacing:0.25rem); use steps 1,2,3,4,6,8,10,12,16,20,24. Semantic padding tokens: public card p-6/p-8, section py-16/py-24; dashboard card p-3/p-4, table cell px-3 py-2, control gap gap-2/gap-3. Density is a token swap via a single density-context attribute on the app-shell (data-density comfortable|compact mapping --pad-card/--row-h/--control-h/--text-body/--gap), never forked stylesheets or ad-hoc pixels. Touch: every interactive element min 44x44pt hit area regardless of visual size. Container max-w 1200px public, fluid dashboard, px-4 mobile gutter rising to px-6/px-8. Add env(safe-area-inset-*) padding tokens for the wrapper shell (notch/home-indicator).

COLOR [must]: semantic OKLCH token layer (no raw palette names in markup): --color-bg, -surface, -surface-muted, -border, -text, -text-muted, -primary (+ -hover/-fg), -accent, plus status --color-success/warning/danger/info each with -subtle (bg tint) + -fg. Primary ~ deep court-teal/padel-blue oklch(0.55 0.13 200) with white -fg; every text-on-bg pair >=4.5:1 (large >=3:1) — Lighthouse a11y gate. Booking-state badges: hold=warning-subtle, paid=info-subtle, confirmed=success-subtle, cancelled=neutral/danger-subtle, no_show=danger-subtle — each ALSO carries an icon/text label (color-blind safe). Caution status colors are calm (not alarm-red) so the refund disclosure is prominent without hurting conversion. Never write bg-green-500 — only semantic utilities so re-theming is one file.

RADIUS & ELEVATION [should]: --radius-sm 6, -md 8 (default control/input), -lg 12 (cards), -xl 16 (modals/sheets), -2xl 20, -full 9999 (pills/slot chips). Shallow Lighthouse-safe elevation: --shadow-xs..xl, each ONE single layer, blur<=8px, low alpha (0.04-0.10). Prefer 1px border + shadow-xs for resting cards; NO box-shadow on frequently re-rendered elements (slot cells, Livewire-refreshed rows — use border + bg tint) to keep the data-dense calendar fast.

COMPONENT SPECS [must] — paired Blade (SSR) + Livewire/Blade (dashboard) primitives, visually identical via shared tokens, full state matrix (default/hover/active/focus-visible/disabled/loading) + visible accessible focus ring. CSP-safe rule: components toggle pre-defined token utility CLASSES via Alpine (:class), NEVER inline :style; hold-timer width via a CSS custom property set through a class. Button: primary/secondary/ghost/danger; sm(h-9 px-3)/md(h-11 px-4)/lg(h-12 px-6); radius-md; weight-medium; focus-visible 2px ring +2px offset; disabled opacity-50; loading inline spinner keeping width (no CLS). Input/select/textarea: h-11, px-3, text-base(16px), radius-md, 1px border, primary focus ring; error=danger border + helper + aria-describedby; label always present. Card: surface bg, radius-lg, border+shadow-sm, header/body/footer slots, two density paddings. Modal: centered desktop, bottom-sheet mobile (radius-xl top), focus-trap, Esc+scrim close, role=dialog/aria-modal.

APP-SHELL LAYER [must]: persistent top bar + bottom-tab-nav (>=56px tall, >=44px tap targets, active/inactive token states) marked wire:persist / outside the wire:navigate swap region so chrome renders once and never repaints during in-app navigation — making 'feels native in TWA/Capacitor' a structural property.

SLOT-GRID / CALENDAR [must]: slot chip radius-md/full, min 44px tall, tabular-nums time label; states via semantic tokens — available (surface+border), selected (primary bg+fg), unavailable (surface-muted+muted+hatch/lock icon, aria-disabled), peak vs off-peak via a price line + subtle accent left-border + 'Peak' label (NOT color alone). Mobile: vertical scroll grouped morning/afternoon/evening with sticky headers. Desktop dashboard: CSS-grid columns=courts, rows=time bands, 1px lines (no shadows), current-time indicator. Sold/held slots keep their box (no reflow). Re-validate server-side on tap.

DISCLOSURE COMPONENT [must]: tokenized, prominent via placement + required affirmative gate on the pay button (not aggressive color), AA caution tokens, compact scannable cancellation-policy tier table reusing data-table tokens.

DARK MODE [nice]: all semantic tokens authored under :root with a .dark override block; @custom-variant dark (&:where(.dark,.dark *)); pre-paint inline script reads localStorage/prefers-color-scheme to avoid flash. Ship light-first v1; enable dark first in the dashboard (honor prefers-color-scheme in the wrapper, drive theme-color/status-bar). Verify dark variants pass AA. Pin Tailwind v4 (v3 config syntax silently won't apply).

topRisks: xenPlatform for-user-id confused-deputy: the platform master Xendit key can act on ANY venue sub-account. Without a single gateway deriving for-user-id strictly from the route-bound authorized venue_id, one buggy/compromised dashboard action can charge or refund another venue's account — the highest-value money-IDOR and currently unnamed in the spec.

Webhook trust: Xendit x-callback-token is a STATIC shared token, not a per-message HMAC. Treating 'signature-verified webhook' as sufficient leaves a leaked/misrouted token able to flip the wrong booking to paid or trigger a fraudulent venue refund. Requires UNIQUE event_id idempotency + amount re-validation + sub-account->venue_id binding + settled-payment check on refunds.

Service worker caching auth/POST/tenant responses or freezing after deploy: in an installed/wrapped app this leaks one tenant's dashboard to the next session or bricks the app on a stale shell with no user 'clear site data'. Mitigated by network-only for /livewire/update + auth + Set-Cookie responses, build-id cache versioning, and an explicit update toast + kill-switch.

Public-surface SSR boundary leak: if the app-shell/SPA model or the SW touches public routes, the site becomes invisible to AI crawlers (GPTBot/ClaudeBot/PerplexityBot run zero JS, no two-wave safety net) and partially to Googlebot — killing the fastest-growing discovery channel. Hard-partition routing + a JS-disabled CI assertion are mandatory.

'Key pages' Lighthouse scope: measuring only the homepage lets the discovery list, venue page, booking calendar, and (steady-state, SW-warmed) dashboard ship unmeasured and silently below 99. The per-route-CLASS 0.99 matrix across all four categories, with steady-state-SPA and SW-warmed dashboard audits, is what makes '99 everywhere' real and verifiable.

CLS regressions are the sneakiest 25%-weight killer: font-swap without metric-matched fallback overrides, images without stored width/height, and Livewire partial re-renders without reserved boxes each silently cost CLS. Requires the metric-matched @font-face deliverable, width/height columns on venue_images, and fixed-box reservation on the slot grid/timer/price.

Apple 4.2 / iOS reality: a remote-origin WebView or thin wrapper is rejected, and iOS Safari doesn't support installable PWA the way Android does. Without committing now to the bundled Capacitor shell + Sanctum Bearer API + OTA + genuine native value, 'release a mobile app fast' is a false promise on iOS and the API surface becomes a costly retrofit.

UU PDP (Law 27/2022, fully enforced, fines up to 2% of revenue): unencrypted phone numbers, no separated WA-marketing consent, no 72-hour breach runbook, and unsecured push device tokens are all live compliance gaps. Phone numbers and tokens are personal data needing encryption, masking, consent separation, and erasure/retention coverage from v1.
